title: ericameria parryi
intro: Ericameria parryi, also known as Parry's goldenrod, is a flowering plant in the daisy family that is native to the southwestern United States. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its essential oils and flavonoids. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and supporting immune function. Ericameria parryi is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, with some herbalists recommending it as a natural remedy for wounds, sore muscles, and respiratory issues.
